@work-leaf read src/terminal_app.rs src/ui.rs src/ui_harness.rs tests/terminal_app.rs tests/ui_harness.rs
I’ll review from the orchestrator-provided bundle only. I’m going to inspect the changed symbols and tests first, then look for behavior regressions introduced by the visual-mode paths.

The patch centers around `TerminalUi` state and the two app wrappers that translate terminal bytes into UI keys. I’ve got the selection model, render highlighting, and yank path loaded; next I’m checking edge cases against the existing input routing and viewport math.

One concrete mismatch is emerging in the left-pane row mapping: selection stores a `left_pane_rows()` index, but the origin uses `control_selected`, which counts only command/agent control rows. I’m checking whether that becomes user-visible when an agent has file/dependency detail rows, because that would make `V`/`y` copy the wrong visible line.

I need one more bit of context around the harness fixture agents to confirm whether existing visual tests cover rows with detail children. If they don’t, the left-pane issue is a patch-scope behavior gap, not just a theoretical style concern.

The fixture does include modified-file and dependency rows, so the row-mapping edge case matters. The current tests assert only that some expected text is present/counts lines, which can miss selecting a neighboring detail row instead of the focused agent row.

I’m also checking the public UI key path, because `TerminalUi::handle_key` is exported while the actual yank text is only available through crate-local wrappers. That matters if this patch leaves direct UI integrations unable to perform the new action.

There’s another origin mismatch on the right command surface: the rendered cursor for non-chat right content is top-left, but the visual origin sync stores the last visible line plus a chat-prompt cursor column. I’m narrowing the review to these two selection-origin issues unless I find something higher impact.

FINDINGS

1. `src/ui.rs:904` starts left-pane visual selections at `row: self.control_selected`, but `control_selected` counts only command/agent rows while `left_pane_rows()` expands extra visible rows for files, conflicts, dependencies, and dependents at `src/ui.rs:733`. `visual_lines_for_selection()` then yanks from that expanded row list at `src/ui.rs:998`, so selecting the second agent after a prior agent has detail rows copies/highlights a previous detail row instead of the focused agent row. In the current harness fixture, pressing `jVy` would start on `files: src/parser.rs`, not the selected `user-2` row.

2. `src/terminal_app.rs:724` and `src/ui_harness.rs:486` always sync the right visual origin with a chat-prompt cursor column. For non-chat right content, `right_cursor_position_with_cursor()` renders the cursor at the top-left (`src/ui.rs:1390`), but `right_text_position()` stores the last visible line plus that column (`src/ui.rs:1610`). Starting `v` on the focused right command/transcript surface therefore selects from a different location than the visible cursor.
NO_FINDINGS
